claims.claim: Anki added FSRS support in 23.10 (October 2023) and enabled it by default for new collections in 24.10+.
claims.note: Inline citation added in both EN and RU articles. Wording differentiates 23.10 introduction vs 24.10 default-on for new collections.

claims.claim: Anki desktop is free and open source; AnkiDroid free; AnkiWeb sync free; AnkiMobile iOS is the only paid piece.
claims.note: Well-established. Article does NOT state the exact AnkiMobile price (intentionally — avoids stale-price risk).

claims.claim: Origa runs on Windows, Linux, macOS, Android, and web; iOS is planned but not shipping.
Origa OCR and STT models run locally (offline card creation).
Origa interface languages: English, Russian, Vietnamese, Korean.
WaniKani is radical-based, English interface, paid subscription.
claims.note: Inline citation added. No exact price stated (intentional).

claims.claim: Bunpro is grammar-focused SRS, web-based, English interface.
Duolingo is gamified and shallow for Japanese.
claims.note: 'Shallow' is an editorial characterization of depth vs dedicated tools, not a factual metric. Defensible but softened. Inline citation added.

claims.claim: Migii is a JLPT test simulator (timed practice).

gate_reason: All competitor claims carry inline external citations. FSRS wording verified against docs.ankiweb.net and accurately differentiates 23.10 introduction vs 24.10 default-on for new collections. RU article typo '食ьба' fixed to '食事'. Both articles are publication-ready. NOTE: this file is a documentation marker for human review; no runtime code enforces it.

brand_voice_check.forbidden_words_in_readme_only: yes (the README lists them as the ban-list — expected)
brand_voice_check.competitor_disparagement: none — every competitor has a 'when it is best' framing
